meaning: adj.不引起兴趣阅读的,难念的,(字迹等)难以认清的,难以理解的
examples: He looked back at the woman for approval, but her face was unreadable.
For some this is the greatest novel in the world. For others it is unreadable.
This makes test cases unreadable and increases maintenance.
Plant genomes, unreadable with many of the current machines, will be rendered clearly.
Handheld devices were offered that were essentially unreadable and impossible to type on.
